mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances indicate expanding household purchasing power that directly fuels corporate sales growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises alongside CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.0 percent annualized, indicating inflation erodes the purchasing power signal.

mechanism_narrative: An annualized CPI reading crossing above 2.5 percent triggers market repricing of tighter monetary policy expectations, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized rate crosses above 2.5 percent
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day prediction window.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive payroll growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and falling unemployment rates amplifies confidence-driven spending shocks that rapidly escalate corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain incremental variance in 60-day forward returns beyond the sum of individual series effects, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index signals capacity expansion and supply chain normalization that reduces input cost pressures, boosting corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS.
